Per-Agent Metrics

Agent Sessions Avg lat (ms) P50 (ms) P95 (ms) P99 ▶ SLA (ms) MCP Timeout% Err Rate% Avg Dur Trend (ms/sess) Unique Tools SLA

✗ = SLA breach  |  ↑ trend = latency degrading over time  |  P99 SLA threshold: {{THRESHOLD_MS}}ms  |  MCP timeout SLA: >5% = breach  |  Error rate SLA: >20% = breach

SLA Violations & Warnings

    {{VIOLATIONS_HTML}}

Systematic Speed Divergences

{{SPEED_FLAGS_HTML}}

Tool Coverage Matrix

{{TOOL_MATRIX_HTML}}

✓ = tool used by agent in at least one session  |  ✗ = never used

Actionable Fixes

High p99 latency: Check network round-trip to LLM endpoint; reduce context window size; enable tool-call batching where supported; consider a faster backend for latency-sensitive agents.

MCP timeout rate >5%: Increase MCP server startup timeout (MCP_CONN_TIMEOUT); confirm MCP stdio server process exits cleanly; check for resource contention on the host; restart MCP servers between sessions.

Session error rate >20%: Review agent logs for common error patterns; verify LLM endpoint credentials; ensure workspace permissions are correct; check for incompatible model versions.

Latency trend degradation: Monitor host memory pressure; restart agent processes periodically; check for context accumulation growing unboundedly; profile tool-call overhead over time.

Tool coverage gap: Verify agent MCP server configuration includes all required servers; run aw replay analyze-divergence for a detailed tool preference matrix; check agent system prompts for tool-use guidance.

ashlr-workbench · session-replay validate-sla Generated {{GENERATED_AT}}
